tasks.title: Audit every public route and classify its response schema
tasks.description: Generate the inventory of all `apps/api/src/routes/**/public/*.ts` route files (exclude index.ts) using `fd`. For each, record the wired `responseSchema` and classify: OK (real entity public-tier schema), LEAK (full base schema), PASSTHROUGH (z.record/z.any/z.unknown), or MISSING (no schema). Write the result to `.qtm/specs/SPEC-210-public-response-tier-enforcement/docs/public-route-audit.md` as a table with a status column. This drives all downstream re-wire work. No code change. Test: N/A (doc artifact); the audit list must be exhaustive (driven by the generated file list, not by hand).

tasks.title: Create SponsorshipLevelPublicSchema
tasks.description: Add `SponsorshipLevelPublicSchema` in `packages/schemas/.../sponsorship-level/*.access.schema.ts` following the `.pick()` tier convention: include only public display fields (id, name, slug, and any public-facing display attributes), EXCLUDE BaseAuditFields (createdById, updatedById, deletedAt, deletedById, createdAt/updatedAt unless intentionally public). Export it. Test: schema unit test asserting a full row parsed through it RETAINS public fields and DROPS audit fields. File: packages/schemas test dir.

tasks.title: Create any other missing public-tier schemas flagged by the audit
tasks.description: For each entity the T-001 audit flagged as LEAK/MISSING that has NO existing `*PublicSchema`, create one in `@repo/schemas` following the `.pick()` convention (no audit/internal fields). Reuse existing public schemas where they exist (e.g. ExchangeRatePublicSchema already exists — no new schema needed there). Test: a schema unit test per new schema. If the audit finds none beyond sponsorship-level, this task closes as N/A with a note.

subtasks.title: List entities needing a new public tier

tasks.title: Add assertConcretePublicSchema startup guard helper
tasks.description: Add a helper (e.g. in apps/api/src/utils/route-factory or response-helpers) `assertConcretePublicSchema(schema, routePath)` that inspects the Zod def and THROWS if the schema is a passthrough/permissive type: z.record, z.any, z.unknown, or a ZodObject in `passthrough` unknownKeys mode. The error message must name the offending route path. Unit test: accepts real tier schemas (AccommodationPublicSchema, etc.); throws on z.record(), z.any(), z.unknown(), and z.object({}).passthrough(). File: apps/api test dir.

subtasks.title: Throw with route path on passthrough
Unit tests: accept tiers, reject passthrough

tasks.title: Make responseSchema required on the public route factory + wire startup guard
tasks.description: In the public route factory(ies) (createPublicRoute, createPublicListRoute, any public variant) under apps/api/src/utils/route-factory*: change the `responseSchema` option from optional to REQUIRED at the type level (omission becomes a TS error). At route construction/registration, call assertConcretePublicSchema(schema, path) so a passthrough/missing schema throws at startup (fail-closed before serving). Do NOT touch protected/admin factories. Test: type-level expectation documented; a registration test that a passthrough schema throws at build.

subtasks.title: responseSchema required in public factory types

tasks.title: Make stripWithSchema fail-closed when no schema is provided
tasks.description: In apps/api/src/utils/response-helpers.ts:90, remove the fail-open branch: when called with no responseSchema, THROW ServiceError(INTERNAL_ERROR, ...) instead of returning raw data. Keep the existing fail-closed-on-parse-error behavior. Audit non-public callers that legitimately omit a schema (if any) and either give them a schema or route them away from strict strip — record findings. Unit test: stripWithSchema throws with no schema; still strips undeclared keys with a .pick() schema; still throws on parse mismatch.

tasks.title: Re-wire accommodation/public/similar.ts to a concrete public schema
tasks.description: Replace `responseSchema: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()))` (similar.ts:29) with `z.array(AccommodationPublicSchema)`. SPEC-187 already strips richDescription there at the data level; this makes the schema concrete so the factory guard accepts it and audit/internal fields are stripped. Regression test: handler returns a row with internal/audit fields → response contains only public card fields. Do not mock the serialization layer.

tasks.title: Re-wire exchange-rates/public/list.ts to ExchangeRatePublicSchema
tasks.description: Before changing: grep apps/web + apps/admin for usage of the fields ExchangeRatePublicSchema would drop (source, expiresAt, audit). If a field is genuinely needed publicly, add it to the public tier deliberately (record the decision in the audit doc); otherwise replace `ExchangeRateSchema` with `ExchangeRatePublicSchema` at list.ts:49. Regression test: response omits source/expiresAt/audit fields, keeps the public rate fields.

subtasks.title: Grep web/admin consumers of dropped fields

tasks.title: Re-wire sponsorship-level/public getById + list to the new public tier
tasks.description: Grep apps/web + apps/admin for the audit fields SponsorshipLevelPublicSchema drops. Then wire SponsorshipLevelPublicSchema (from T-002) into sponsorship-level/public/getById.ts and list.ts (replacing SponsorshipLevelSchema). Regression tests for both routes: createdById/updatedById/deletedAt/deletedById absent, public fields present.

subtasks.title: Wire public tier in getById + list

tasks.title: Fix every remaining route flagged by the audit
tasks.description: For each LEAK/PASSTHROUGH/MISSING route from T-001 not already handled by T-007/8/9: grep web/admin consumers of fields that would be dropped, then wire the correct entity public-tier schema (reusing or creating via T-003). Add a per-route regression test asserting leak fields absent. Record each decision in the audit doc. If the list is large, keep tasks atomic per route group; flag for /replan if it exceeds a single focused unit.

subtasks.title: Grep consumers + wire public tier each

tasks.title: Add boot-the-public-router fail-closed test
tasks.description: Add a test that registers the real public router and asserts it builds WITHOUT throwing (all public routes now have concrete tier schemas), and a negative test that a deliberately-passthrough public route throws at registration via assertConcretePublicSchema. This is the runtime backstop that makes a missed route fail loudly instead of leaking. File: apps/api test dir.

subtasks.title: Negative: passthrough route throws at boot

tasks.title: Full regression sweep across api, web, admin
tasks.description: Run full typecheck + lint + test for apps/api, and typecheck for apps/web and apps/admin (consumers of the tightened responses). All green. Confirm the public-route-audit.md is fully resolved (every route OK/fixed). Confirm no consumer broke from a dropped field (if one did, it was added to its public tier deliberately in T-008/9/10). Report real exit codes.

tasks.title: Document the public-response contract rule
tasks.description: Document in apps/api/docs/route-architecture.md (and/or the API CLAUDE.md) the enforced rule: every public route MUST declare a concrete entity public-tier responseSchema; the factory rejects missing/passthrough schemas at startup; stripWithSchema is fail-closed. Note BaseModel still does SELECT * by design and the response tier is the single enforcement boundary. Reference SPEC-210.
